name: Trenches of the Bellmunt d'Urgell hills - Circular Route
name_original: Trincheras de las colinas de Bellmunt d'Urgell - Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Bellmunt D'Urgell offers varied landscapes, characterized by its elevated position within the Serra de Bellmunt. The region provides extensive panoramic views, earning it the nickname "Balcony of Urgell." Terrain includes hills, ponds, and areas of forest, with routes ranging from easy, accessible paths to more challenging trails. Best hiking trails around Bellmunt D'Urgell The most popular hiking route is Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This circular route offers continuous lake views and opportunities for birdwatching. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trenches of the Bellmunt d'Urgell hills - Circular Route, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This trail leads through the Bellmunt d'Urgell hills. Local hikers also love the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond loop from Aparcament Estany d'Ivars i Vilasana Oest, a 6.7 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the pond, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Hiking around Bellmunt D'Urgell is defined by elevated viewpoints, hills, and pond-side paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Bellmunt D'Urgell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Bellmunt D'Urgell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Bellmunt D'Urgell?
A: Bellmunt D'Urgell is known as the 'Balcony of Urgell' due to its elevated position, offering extensive panoramic views of the Pyrenees and the Plana d'Urgell. You'll find varied terrain including hills, tranquil pond-side paths like those around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;, and areas of forest. The region is part of a Natural Interest Plan, ensuring diverse natural beauty.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Bellmunt D'Urgell?
A: Yes, Bellmunt D'Urgell offers several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936941597/trenches-of-the-bellmunt-d-urgell-hills-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trenches of the Bellmunt d'Urgell hills - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.2 km path. The area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt; also features easy, circular routes, perfect for a relaxed walk with continuous lake views.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bellmunt D'Urgell?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Bellmunt D'Urgell listed on komoot, ranging from easy walks to more moderate excursions. This variety ensures options for different ability levels and preferences.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Bellmunt D'Urgell?
A: The trails in Bellmunt D'Urgell are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning panoramic views, the peaceful atmosphere of the pond-side walks, and the diverse terrain that keeps routes interesting.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Bellmunt D'Urgell?
A: Yes, many routes in Bellmunt D'Urgell are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938197658/ivars-and-vila-sana-pond-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936941597/trenches-of-the-bellmunt-d-urgell-hills-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trenches of the Bellmunt d'Urgell hills - Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, both offering convenient starting and ending points.

Q: Can I find any cultural or historical attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, some routes combine natural exploration with cultural experiences. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penelles Street Art Murals – Penelles (village of murals) loop from Penelles&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the village of Penelles, known for its vibrant street art. You can also visit the Gothic-Renaissance Church of Sant Josep in Bellmunt D'Urgell itself.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or natural landmarks to look out for?
A: Bellmunt D'Urgell is famous for its panoramic views. The Mirador del carrer del Calvari offers extensive vistas of the Pyrenees and the Plana d'Urgell. The region research also mentions the Bellmunt Sanctuary and the Cingles de Tavertet for spectacular 'bird's eye views'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt; is another significant natural landmark, ideal for birdwatching.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Bellmunt D'Urgell dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in Bellmunt D'Urgell are suitable for dogs, especially the more open and rural paths. The circular routes around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt; are generally popular with dog walkers. Always ensure your dog is under control and check for any specific local regulations or signage regarding pets.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bellmunt D'Urgell?
A: The varied terrain of Bellmunt D'Urgell makes it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, while summer can be enjoyable, especially for early morning or late afternoon hikes. Winter hikes are also possible, particularly on the lower elevation trails, though conditions can vary.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant forests to explore?
A: In the vicinity of the Bellmunt Sanctuary, you can discover magnificent beech woods and hidden waterfalls such as the Tombo del Mir and the Salt del Molí falls. The Grevolosa, a natural sanctuary with monumental trees, is also nearby, offering a chance to experience lush forest environments.

Q: What are some nearby attractions I can visit after a hike?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several interesting sites. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de Almenara Tower&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of San Vicente de Almenara&lt;/a&gt; for historical insights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5197404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penelles Street Art Murals&lt;/a&gt; offer a unique artistic experience, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt; is excellent for birdwatching.

Q: Is there public transport or parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific public transport options to every trailhead might be limited, the main villages and attractions often have parking facilities. For example, there is an Aparcament Estany d'Ivars i Vilasana Oest which serves as a starting point for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond loop from Aparcament Estany d'Ivars i Vilasana Oest&lt;/a&gt;. It's advisable to check local transport schedules or plan for car access when exploring the region.
tips: These trenches were defense lines, built between April and December 1938, to prevent Franco's advance.
The name of the spring comes from how bitter its water was, due to its high amount of calcium. Formerly, the water from the source had been used for animals or to irrigate crops.
This house is named after the Ral road, which is the name given to royal roads before there were roads. This house was used to give rest or relief to the animals of those who traveled that way.
